case.paper_id: Extract or assign a unique case ID. Example: Bruner_ExpEco_2017_amYY.
case.paper_title: Extract the original paper title. Example: Does decision error decrease with risk aversion?
case.study_type: Extract the study type. Example: experimental, observational, survey, meta-analysis, other, or not_stated.
case.focal_claim: Copy the focal claim being tested. Example: The likelihood of decision error should decrease with the degree of risk aversion.
case.case_mode: Use the execution mode. Example: planned_path_execution.

original_datasets.name: Dataset name used for the robustness reanalysis. Example: RiskData.
original_datasets.filename: Exact data file name. Example: RiskData.dta.
original_datasets.file_format: File format. Example: csv, dta, rdata, rds, xlsx, json, parquet.
original_datasets.columns: List key variable names if available. Example: [participant_id, PV_choice, RV_choice, LV_score]. If unknown, use not_stated.

summary_statistics.info: Structural metadata if available, such as rows, columns, and column types. If unknown, use not_stated.
summary_statistics.describe: Descriptive statistics if available. If not available, use null.

access.url: Data URL or repository link if available. If local only, give the local path or not_stated.
access.restrictions: Access restriction or license. Example: public, restricted, request_required, or not_stated.

original_datasets.notes: Any caveats about the data, such as missing metadata, unclear variables, or required preprocessing.

planned_method.planned_id: Unique ID for this planned analysis. Example: Bruner_ExpEco_2017_amYY_path01.

tasks.task_role: Use conclusion_oriented_reanalysis for Task1 or comparable_result_oriented_reanalysis for Task2 when appropriate.
tasks.task_instruction: Instruction for this task. Example: Conduct the analysis without additional restriction. Or: Do not use rank-order tests.

analysis_path.path_name: Short name for the path. Example: linear_regression_risk_aversion_decision_error.
analysis_path.path_description: Concise description of what should be run. Example: Compute risk aversion from PV and RV tasks, compute decision error from LV task, then regress decision error on risk aversion.
analysis_path.software: Software or language used. Example: R, Stata, Matlab, Python, or not_stated.
analysis_path.model_family: Main model or test family. Example: linear_regression, logistic_regression, correlation, t_test, poisson, multilevel, Bayesian, or not_stated.
preprocessing_steps: List ordered preprocessing and preparation steps. Example: Import original data.

key_choices.outcome_measure: How the outcome is measured or constructed. Example: Decision error score from the LV task.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: How the main predictor is measured or constructed. Example: Average safe choices from PV and RV tasks as risk aversion score.
key_choices.sample_restriction: Sample inclusion or exclusion rule, or null.
key_choices.data_processing: Any reshaping, recoding, aggregation, scaling, or transformation.
key_choices.model_choice: Main model or statistical test used. Example: linear regression.
key_choices.inference_rule: Rule used to judge support. Example: p-value threshold, robust standard error, Bayesian rule, or null.

outcome.name: Outcome variable name or constructed outcome. Example: decision_error.
outcome.definition: Definition of the outcome from data. Example: Decision error score derived from the LV task.

main_predictor.name: Main predictor variable name. Example: risk_aversion_score.
main_predictor.definition: Definition of the predictor from data. Example: Average number of safe choices from PV and RV tasks.

analysis_code.available: true if analysis code is available; false otherwise.
code_files: Files needed for this specific task. Example: analysis.R.
analysis_code.entry_file: Main script to run, if known. Example: analysis.R. If unknown, use null.
analysis_code.run_command: Command to run the task, if known. Example: Rscript analysis.R. If unknown, use null.
analysis_code.notes: Task specific notes about running the analysis code.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Task-specific rule. Example: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

files.file_name: Describe each available code file and how it relates to execution. Example: analysis.R: Runs the regression analysis. ONLY include files TO BE EXECUTED HERE. YOU MUST ENSURE THAT NECESSARY FILES FOR EXECUTION EXIST BEFORE FILLING OUT THIS TEMPLATE.

codebase.notes: Overall code notes, such as unclear entry file, dependency issues, or folder structure.

docker_specs.base_image: Base Docker image. Example: rocker/verse or python:3.10.

python: Python packages with versions, if any. Example: pandas==2.0.3.
r: R packages with versions, if any. Example: dplyr, haven, broom.
other: Other system tools. Example: git, make, wget.

hardware.gpu_support: true if GPU is required; false otherwise.
hardware.min_gpu_memory_gb: Minimum GPU memory if needed, otherwise null.
hardware.min_ram_gb: Minimum RAM if known, otherwise null.

analysis.instructions: High-level execution instruction. Example: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset and produce structured execution outputs.
target_task_ids: Task IDs to execute. Example: Task1, Task2.
allowed_actions: Allowed actions. Example: inspect data files, inspect code files, run provided code, make bounded implementation fixes, write compact result extraction scripts.
not_allowed_actions: Disallowed actions. Example: change the focal claim, change the dataset, replace the planned path with a different analysis path, choose a more favorable result.

debugging_rule.repair_scope: Allowed repair scope. Example: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Expected metrics or outputs for later comparison. Example: coefficient, p-value, test statistic, Cohen's d, conclusion text.
